channel.title: Scampedia — Latest Scams
channel.description: The living, AI-updated encyclopedia of scams — new entries as they're documented.

item.title: Video Call Deepfake Extortion Scam
item.description: A stranger video calls you out of nowhere (FaceTime, WhatsApp, or similar), keeps you on briefly while secretly capturing your face, then contacts you later with an AI-manipulated image designed to falsely implicate you in something serious — demanding payment to keep it quiet.

item.title: AI Voice Clone Scam
item.description: Using just a few seconds of audio from social media, scammers clone a loved one's voice with AI and call you claiming to be in an emergency. The voice sounds completely real.

item.title: Deepfake Celebrity Endorsement Scam
item.description: AI-generated video or audio shows a celebrity or public figure appearing to endorse an investment platform, giveaway, or product they have never actually endorsed, used to lend false credibility to a scam.

item.title: AI Chatbot Romance Scam
item.description: An AI-powered chatbot, sometimes on a dating app, carries on a convincingly personal conversation for weeks or months before steering the victim toward sending money, gifts, or investing in a fraudulent scheme.

item.title: Virtual Kidnapping Scam
item.description: A caller claims to be holding a family member hostage and demands an immediate ransom payment, sometimes using an AI-cloned voice of the "victim" crying for help, even though the person is actually safe.

item.title: QR Code Scam
item.description: A malicious QR code — placed over a legitimate one on a parking meter, menu, or poster, or sent digitally — redirects victims to a fake payment or login page designed to steal money or credentials.

item.title: Fake Trading App Scam
item.description: Victims are convinced to download a fraudulent investment or trading app that shows fabricated, ever-increasing account balances, but withdrawals are blocked or additional "fees" are demanded before any money can be returned.

item.title: Grandparent Emergency Scam
item.description: Scammers call pretending to be your grandchild in trouble — arrested, in an accident, or at the hospital — and urgently need bail money or medical fees sent via gift cards or wire transfer.

item.title: Unemployment Benefits Identity Theft Scam
item.description: Criminals use stolen personal information to file fraudulent unemployment claims in a victim's name, often surfacing only when the real person receives a tax form for benefits they never applied for or received.

item.title: Package Delivery Text Scam
item.description: A text message claims a package couldn't be delivered due to an unpaid fee or incomplete address, with a link to a fake carrier site designed to steal payment card details.

item.title: Crypto Investment Scam
item.description: Promises of guaranteed high returns on cryptocurrency or forex investments, often introduced through social media, fake apps, or romance relationships. Victims lose everything when they try to withdraw.

item.title: Fake Bank Fraud Alert Scam
item.description: A text or call appears to come from your bank warning of suspicious activity and asks you to "verify" your account by providing your card number, PIN, or a one-time passcode — information a real bank never requests this way.

item.title: Pig Butchering Scam
item.description: Named for the practice of fattening livestock before slaughter, scammers build a long-term relationship — often starting as a romance or friendship — before gradually persuading the victim to invest increasing amounts into a fake trading platform, ultimately taking everything.

item.title: Medicare / Health Insurance Scam
item.description: Callers pretend to be from Medicare or a health insurance company offering free equipment or coverage updates, then steal your Medicare number to commit fraud and bill for services you never received.

item.title: Social Security Number Suspended Scam
item.description: An automated call or voicemail claims your Social Security number has been "suspended" due to suspicious activity and instructs you to press a number or call back, aiming to extract your SSN and other personal details.

item.title: SIM Swap Scam
item.description: A scammer convinces your mobile carrier to transfer your phone number to a SIM card they control, letting them intercept the text-message codes used to reset passwords and drain bank or crypto accounts.

item.title: "Can You Hear Me?" Robocall Scam
item.description: A robocall or live scammer opens with a simple yes-or-no question like "can you hear me?" hoping to record you saying "yes," which they claim can later be used to authorize charges you never agreed to.

item.title: Smishing Text Message Scam
item.description: Phishing conducted over SMS text message, often impersonating a delivery service, bank, or government agency with a link to a fake site designed to steal login credentials or payment information.

item.title: Tech Support Pop-Up Scam
item.description: A scary browser pop-up claims your computer is infected with viruses and displays a 'Microsoft' or 'Apple' phone number. Calling it connects you to scammers who request remote access to your computer.

item.title: Wangiri / One-Ring Callback Scam
item.description: A call rings once from an unfamiliar international number and hangs up before you can answer, banking on curiosity making you call back. The returned call connects to a premium-rate line that racks up enormous per-minute charges, a cut of which goes to the scammer.

item.title: Social Media Account Takeover Scam
item.description: A hacked or cloned social media account messages the victim's friends and family asking for money, gift cards, or a "favor" verification code, exploiting the trust of an existing relationship.

item.title: Fake IRS Tax Refund Scam
item.description: Calls, texts, or emails claiming you owe back taxes and will be arrested, or that you are owed a refund that requires you to pay a processing fee first — often via gift cards.

item.title: Student Loan Forgiveness Scam
item.description: Companies advertise guaranteed student loan forgiveness or dramatically reduced payments for an upfront fee, then either do nothing, enroll borrowers in a plan they could have gotten for free, or steal the login credentials used to "help."

item.title: Sextortion Scam
item.description: A scammer coerces or tricks a victim into sharing intimate photos or video, then threatens to release them to friends, family, or publicly unless paid, often escalating demands after the first payment.

item.title: Fake Online Store Scam
item.description: A convincing but fraudulent online store, often advertised through social media, offers heavily discounted name-brand products, takes payment, and either ships nothing, a counterfeit, or a completely different item.

item.title: Jury Duty Scam
item.description: A caller claims to be a sheriff or court officer saying you missed jury duty and now face arrest unless you immediately pay a fine — usually via gift cards or a prepaid card — to avoid being taken into custody.

item.title: Online Marketplace Buyer Scam
item.description: A "buyer" on a marketplace app sends a fake payment confirmation screenshot or overpays with a bad check, then asks the seller to ship the item or refund the difference before the payment actually clears.

item.title: USCIS / Immigration Scam
item.description: Scammers posing as immigration officials contact applicants claiming a fee must be paid immediately by phone to avoid deportation or a denied application, exploiting fear around immigration status.

item.title: Fake Rental Listing Scam
item.description: A rental listing — sometimes copied from a real property that isn't actually for rent — advertises an attractive price and asks for a deposit or first month's rent before the "landlord," who is never available to meet in person, disappears.

item.title: Fake Job Offer Scam
item.description: A recruiter offers a remote job with little to no interview, then asks the "new hire" to pay for training materials, equipment, or a background check upfront, or to deposit a check and wire back the difference.
